original_abstract: Abstract Nowadays public policymakers are offered with opportunities to take data-driven evidence-based decisions by analyzing the very large volumes of policy-related data that are generated through different channels (e.g., e-services, mobile apps, social media). Machine learning (ML) and artificial intelligence (AI) tehcnologies ease and automate the analysis of large policy-related datasets, which helps policymakers to realize a shift toward data-driven decisions. Nevertheless, the deployment and use of AI tools for public policy development is also associated with significant technical, political, and operation challenges. For instance, AI-based policy development solutions must be transparent and explainable to policymakers, while at the same time adhering to the mandates of emerging regulations such as the AI Act of the European Union. This paper introduces some of the main technical, operational, regulatory compliance challenges of AI-based policymaking. Accordingly, it introduces technological solutions for overcoming them, including: (i) a reference architecture for AI-based policy development, (ii) a virtualized cloud-based tool for the specification and implementation of ML-based data-driven policies, (iii) a ML framework that enables the development of transparent and explainable ML models for policymaking, and (iv) a set of guidelines for using the introduced technical solutions to achieve regulatory compliance. The paper ends up illustrating the validation and use of the introduced solutions in real-life public policymaking cases for various local governments.
humanized_abstract: POLICY DEVELOPMENT 4.0: ADDRESSING PRACTICAL CHALLENGES OF AI-BASED PUBLIC POLICY DEVELOPMENT Abstract Nowadays public policymakers are provided with numerous choices from which to select the data and analysis that will support their decisions. The reason for this is that public policymakers have unparalleled access to a vast quantity of policy-related data from numerous, diverse information sources. Open data, for example, is available in almost all municipalities. The use of e-services and mobile apps is also increasingly common. In addition, social media is a popular platform that is widely used by individuals, organizations, and governments. Machine learning (ML) and artificial intelligence (AI) tecnologies ease and automate the process of analysing the large amounts of policy-related data at their disposal, thereby facilitating a data-driven policymaking process. Nevertheless, the use of AI tools in the public policy development process is also fraught with technical, political and operational challenges. In order to give an example, the Explainability of the decision-making process of an AI-based policy development solution is important for policymakers. In order to address the mandated requirements of emerging regulations such as the AI Act of the European Union, the regulatory compliance of AI-based policy development solutions must also be ensured. This paper briefly presents the technical, operational and regulatory compliance challenges of AI-based policymaking and the technological solutions for tackling them. It also shows a reference architecture for AI-based policy development, a virtualized cloud-based tool for policy specification and for the implementation of ML-based data-driven policies, a ML framework for developing transparent and explainable ML models for policymaking, and a set of guidelines for ensuring regulatory compliance when using the solutions introduced in the paper. The validation and use of the solutions introduced in the paper in public policymaking for various municipalities are also shown in the paper.
